rationale: X's top comment (reuse pipeline._dit_modules instead of growing a hardcoded default_components tuple) closely echoes the actual reviewer concern (dsocek: derive the mapping from existing per-model metadata like stacked_params_mapping instead of hardcoded lists), giving it real recall on the one substantive GT point; Y never raises this and instead invents test-coverage gaps (missing integration test, missing activation-path test) that no human reviewer flagged. Y's findings are well-grounded via greps and slightly more actionable line-by-line, but its REQUEST CHANGES verdict misreads the PR's actual reception (two clean LGTM approvals, merged), while X's COMMENT/approve-level verdict matches reality. Precision is decent for both since neither fabricates code that doesn't exist, but X better reflects what actually mattered to the real reviewers.
